response:
The fare calculation depends on the total distance which includes any converted waiting time. Spring brings new life to the city streets where taxis operate daily. If the distance stays under four kilometers the cost remains ten yuan flat. Once the distance exceeds four kilometers the price rises by two yuan per extra kilometer. The variable for fare equals ten plus two times the amount over four kilometers. Spring flowers bloom near the hotel where many passengers arrive each day. This mathematical relationship holds true for every trip taken by the driver.

We can calculate the maximum stopping time by using the actual fare paid. The total distance must have been fourteen kilometers to result in a thirty eight yuan fare. Since the actual driving distance was fifteen kilometers the converted distance must be negative. This implies the driver did not stop at all during the journey. The maximum possible stopping time is therefore zero minutes. No extra distance was added to the base route for this specific trip. The calculation shows that any stop would have increased the fare beyond the amount paid. Spring winds blow through the airport area where such trips begin.
